wallet.name: Wallet
wallet.description:
The wallet is worked from thick full-grain leather, hand-stitched along the edges with waxed thread the color of strong tea. The dye is uneven in the way of good hides, not factory perfect. A maker's stamp inside the flap is too worn to read. It sits heavy in your palm, the sort of piece a man buys once and carries for years.

You count what it holds. Twenty dollars in worn notes, nothing more. No credit cards. No identification. The pockets are otherwise empty.

contents.examineNote: Examining the pockets carefully, you identify a small piece of carefully folded paper tucked deep into a corner seam.

wallet_slip.name: Slip of Paper
wallet_slip.description: A small slip of paper, folded until the creases have gone soft. The ink is faded but legible.

lard.name: Lard
lard.description: A wide-mouth mason jar packed with smooth, ivory-white rendered lard. The waxed lid is screwed down tight, and a faint waxy scent rises when you tilt it toward the light.

lamp_oil.name: Lamp Oil
lamp_oil.description: A small mason jar, half full of pale golden lamp oil. The glass is wick-yellow at the shoulder where oil has crept up the sides. The cork stopper is stained and fits tight.
lamp_oil.alternateDescription: A small mason jar, half full of pale golden lamp oil. The glass is wick-yellow at the shoulder where oil has crept up the sides. The cork stopper is stained and fits tight, though a crude hole has been bored through the top where someone used a knife tip to pierce it.

hunting_knife.name: Hunting Knife
hunting_knife.description: A fixed-blade hunting knife in a dark leather sheath scuffed from carry. The wooden handle is smooth where a palm has worn it. When you ease the blade free, the edge catches the bare bulb's light—recently honed, and kept close at hand.

fedora.name: Fedora
fedora.description: A weathered felt fedora, dark charcoal gone dusty at the brim. The grosgrain ribbon is stained where smoke and rain have worked the same habit. The crown is dented from carry and sleep. It smells faintly of cigarettes and another room.

mens_cologne.name: Men's Cologne
mens_cologne.description: A heavy glass bottle of men's cologne with a ground stopper and a label printed in restrained copperplate. The scent is cedar, bergamot, and something drier beneath—tobacco leaf, perhaps, or the memory of it. The Ridge Haberdashery wraps its goods as though altitude might bruise them.

straight_razor.name: Straight Razor
straight_razor.description: A straight razor in a fitted leather sheath, the blade hollow-ground and polished to a mirror that shows your face in fragments. The handle is horn, warm from the maker's bench, pinned with a brass tang. The edge is sharp enough to part a hair without complaint.

hair_tonic.name: Hair Tonic
hair_tonic.description: A cobalt bottle of hair tonic with a cork sealed in red wax. The label promises restorative virtue in language more medical than honest. The liquid smells of bay rum and barber soap. A man's vanity in a bottle small enough to hide in a coat pocket.

shaving_brush.name: Shaving Brush
shaving_brush.description: A shaving brush with a turned handle of pale boxwood and a knot of badger hair dense enough to hold a proper lather. The ferrule is nickel silver, stamped with a London maker's mark worn thin at the edge. Used with care, it would last longer than most resolutions on the High Line.

cuff_links.name: Cuff Links
cuff_links.description: A pair of cuff links in a small velvet tray: onyx faces set in sterling, engine-turned at the border so they catch lamplight without shouting. The posts click shut with the satisfying precision of good mechanism. The sort of detail a man buys when he wants a room to revise its estimate of him.

bow_tie.name: Bow Tie
bow_tie.description: A bow tie of black silk grosgrain, pre-tied on a band but finished well enough that the deception would pass at a distance. The fabric has a dry hand and a subtle rib that speaks of formal rooms and better lighting than the ridge usually affords.

custom_shirt.name: Custom Men's Shirt
custom_shirt.description: A measurement card and fabric swatch pinned to a tailor's ticket for a custom shirt: long-grain cotton, mother-of-pearl buttons, collar cut to your measure. The haberdasher notes a six-week lead time and, with quiet pride, Savile Row construction. The garment itself is not here yet. The promise is.

custom_trousers.name: Custom Men's Trousers
custom_trousers.description: A tailor's order for custom trousers: waist, inseam, and rise recorded in ink that does not smudge. The cloth sample is worsted wool, dark enough for church and boardrooms that do not exist on the High Line but might in Graysmill. Six weeks, the card says. Made on Savile Row in London.

custom_jacket.name: Custom Men's Jacket
custom_jacket.description: A commission card for a custom jacket: shoulder width, chest, and lapel style noted with the care of a man who believes fit is morality. The fabric sample is long-grain wool in charcoal, substantial enough to hold a ridge wind at bay. Six weeks' lead. Designed and constructed on Savile Row in London, as though the address itself were a kind of guarantee.

mens_pea_coat.name: Men's Pea Coat
mens_pea_coat.description: A men's pea coat of heavy navy melton wool, double-breasted with anchor buttons and a collar that can be turned against wind that has never learned manners. The lining is quilted. The shoulders are broad. It smells of new cloth and cold air, as though it has been waiting for a ship that docks at altitude.

mens_duster.name: Men's Duster
mens_duster.description: A full-length men's duster in oiled canvas the color of winter road dust, with a split back for riding and pockets deep enough to lose a man's intentions. The metal buttons are darkened against tarnish. It is less garment than declaration: a man who expects distance, weather, and the kind of work that keeps his hands dirty.

wool_fabric_sample.name: Wool Fabric Sample
wool_fabric_sample.description: A small cut of premium long-grain wool, one hundred percent, folded into a card and tied with twine. The hand is dense and cool, the weave tight enough that light passes reluctantly. The haberdasher's note on the card reads simply: for consideration.

mining_pick.name: Mining Pick
mining_pick.description: A prospector's pick with a hickory handle darkened by pitch and honest sweat. The head is forged steel, double-bit, with an edge that has been dressed recently on a wheel. Weighted for mountain ground: quartz, shale, and the stubborn ribs of ore that men chase above the timberline.

shovel.name: Shovel
shovel.description: A round-point shovel with a long ash handle and a steel blade rolled at the edge. The socket is riveted true, the grip worn smooth where working hands have found the balance. Built for snow, gravel, and the endless digging that altitude demands.

garden_rake.name: Garden Rake
garden_rake.description: A bow rake with sixteen forged tines and a handle cut from straight-grained ash. The head is pinned, not merely nailed, the way tools were made when men expected to keep them through more than one hard season.

hand_drill.name: Hand Drill
hand_drill.description: A carpenter's brace-and-bit hand drill in beech and steel, the chuck tight, the crank worn bright at the palm. Three interchangeable bits ride in a cloth wrap: steel made for timber, cabinet work, and the small precise holes hardware men trust more than luck.

blacksmith_hammer.name: Blacksmith's Hammer
blacksmith_hammer.description: A cross-peen blacksmith's hammer with a hickory handle shaped to the swing. The head carries the honest scars of work, peened and polished where countless blows have landed true. Not a wall decoration. A tool meant to move hot iron.

mid_anvil.name: Mid-Sized Anvil
mid_anvil.description: A mid-sized cast-steel anvil, roughly eighty pounds, with a clean face, hard horn, and a hardy hole dark from use. The waist is stamped with a Pittsburgh maker's mark. It rings when you tap it, a bright note that says the steel was tempered by someone who understood the trade.

box_of_nails.name: Box of Nails
box_of_nails.description: A pine box of cut common nails, four-penny weight, the heads slightly proud, the points sharp enough to bite on first strike. The lid is chalk-marked with the count and the date received. The small arithmetic of every cabin, fence, and mine brace on the ridge.

copper_wire_spool.name: Spool of Copper Wire
copper_wire_spool.description: A small spool of bare eighteen-gauge copper wire wound tight on a turned wooden core. The strand is fine as a pencil lead, rose-bright and springy, the sort of wire used for lantern leads, telegraph repairs, and the small electrical improvisations men make when the valley is too far below. The spool is light in the hand and the coils sit close, kept dry against mountain damp.
copper_wire_spool.alternateDescription: A small spool of bare eighteen-gauge copper wire wound tight on a turned wooden core. The strand is fine as a pencil lead, rose-bright and springy, the sort of wire used for lantern leads, telegraph repairs, and the small electrical improvisations men make when the valley is too far below. The spool is light in the hand and the coils sit close, kept dry against mountain damp. Only a small length has been unwound and cut away; plenty of wire remains on the spool.

wick.name: Wick
wick.description: A short wick improvised from a twist of dense wool fabric wrapped around a fine strand of bare copper wire. The wool drinks oil readily; the wire gives the bundle enough spine to stand upright in a narrow opening. Crude work, but serviceable.

crude_lantern.name: Crude Lantern
crude_lantern.description: A makeshift lantern built from the mason jar of lamp oil, its cork stopper pierced to admit a wool-and-copper wick. No chimney, no brasswork—only glass, oil, and patience. When you strike a match to the wick, the flame catches unevenly but holds, throwing a small honest circle of light at your feet.
